Event_Description: Noah is a barista apprentice at a specialty coffee shop, methodically working through the craft fundamentals — pour-over, espresso dial-in, grinder settings, microfoam, cold brew, and TDS readings. He is technical and detail-led, asking precise variable-by-variable questions and genuinely excited about the science of a good cup. He loves geeking out over single-origin beans and processing methods, and supplements his bench work with barista training manuals and coffee-culture reading to grow beyond pure technique.
core_event: Across a structured apprenticeship arc, Noah moves from pour-over and espresso basics through cold brew and microfoam, then single-origin dial-in, water quality, and batch brewing — culminating in a signature-blend capstone — and follows the bench work with deeper study of specialty-coffee training material.

Event_Description: Noah begins his apprenticeship at a specialty coffee shop and wants to learn the café’s staple drinks and commonly used beans first.
